task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE Supervisor GUI window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Simulated Multiparameter Monitor is active and displaying Heart Rate
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within OpenICE, a 'Simulated Multiparameter Monitor' device is selected and active. A green number representing the Heart Rate (HR) is clearly visible on the device display.

task_completion.description: The ocr_data directory is created on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows the existence of the directory 'ocr_data' located at '~/Desktop/ocr_data/'.

task_completion.description: Three cropped images of the HR number are saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The files 'hr_sample_1.png', 'hr_sample_2.png', and 'hr_sample_3.png' are visible in the 'ocr_data' directory. Evidence of the images being small crops of the green HR number should be visible (e.g., in an image viewer, screenshot tool, or file manager thumbnails).

task_completion.description: Three corresponding text files with integer values are saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The files 'hr_sample_1.txt', 'hr_sample_2.txt', and 'hr_sample_3.txt' are visible in the 'ocr_data' directory. A text editor or terminal (e.g., using 'cat' or 'echo') should show that these files contain integer numbers corresponding to the HR values.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenICE GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenICE Supervisor application must be visibly running on the desktop. The agent should not have bypassed the application by just creating dummy files.

integrity.description: Images were genuinely captured and cropped from the screen
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot tool, image editor (like GIMP or KolourPaint), or selection UI being used to capture and crop the specific HR number from the OpenICE window.
Look for a text editor or terminal commands being used to manually input the observed HR integers into the text files, rather than a script generating random numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset directory must be exactly '/home/ga/Desktop/ocr_data'. The expected output is exactly 3 pairs of files: 'hr_sample_1.png'/'.txt', 'hr_sample_2.png'/'.txt', and 'hr_sample_3.png'/'.txt'. The images must be cropped (dimensions between 20x20 and 400x400 pixels) focusing specifically on the green Heart Rate (HR) number from the Simulated Multiparameter Monitor in OpenICE. The text files must contain only the integer heart rate value corresponding to the image.
